title: [phase:critique] [family:ui-runtime-review] [provider_hint:subagent] Review the current Happy production resolution. Facts: production /happy/ serves updated HTML with Live Session wired to /cockpit; /cockpit returns 200; unauthenticated GET /api/happy/state,/events,/task-detail return 401 by design...
summary_text: - **Medium**: Cleanup-on-signal can become destructive if it runs before snapshot/setup state is fully initialized. In this script, `buildRestoreSql(null)` deletes the smoke user row, so a signal before `originalUser` is captured could remove a legitimate row. Add state guards like `snapshotReady` and `setupApplied` before allowing signal cleanup, and skip restore/delete if not ready ([smoke-happy-login.js](/Users/masayuki/Dev/cloudflare-workers-hub-deploy/scripts/smoke-happy-login.js)). - **Medium**: `runD1` in signal handlers can stall termination because it retries/sleeps and has no subprocess timeout. In `SIGTERM` paths this may hang shutdown. Use a “fast cleanup mode” (single attempt, short `spawnSync` timeout) for signal/exception handlers ([smoke-happy-login.js](/Users/masayuki/Dev/cloudflare-workers-hub-deploy/scripts/smoke-happy-login.js)). - **Low**: `uncaughtException`/`unhandledRejection` handlers must wrap cleanup in `try/catch`; otherwise cleanup errors can mask the original failure and produce noisy double-fault exits. Keep idempotent guard atomic and set `process.exitCode` once.
